understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level by moving a pushable object (block) onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', using the available selectable pieces, within the per-level step budget, until the engine reported WIN and the full game was completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement operated on a grid where each move shifts the currently selected piece by exactly one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable object was immediately adjacent in the direction of movement and the space beyond it was free, the selected piece could push it one cell; otherwise movement was blocked. Walls/obstacles prevented movement and could create blocked moves; the movement_blocked flags were only hints and not guaranteed outcomes. When multiple selectable pieces existed, CLICK with an exact pixel/grid coordinate of another selectable switched control to that piece.
